## prompt-draft-bill

*Generated by Process A from `auto-bill-01/sub-prompts/prompt-5-draft-bill.md`
and `cancer-automated/.../VVUQ-05/draft-bill/prompt-draft-bill.md`, adapted for
the single-prompt v5.0 financial-data build.*

Write the H. R. 9510 Bill v5.0 draft (scaffold) LaTeX into
`auto-bill-02/draft-bill`, building from the Bill v3.0 measure in
`cancer-automated/.../papers/VVUQ-05/final-bill` (no `/deliverables`) and using
less numbered and lettered lists. Preserve the look of professional text-based
diagrams and tables exactly as they render in GitHub Markdown. No PNG or JPG
images; the permitted media are full-width white-background tables, centered
monospace ASCII figures, and gray-scale Mermaid diagrams reproduced in LaTeX as
gray-scale TikZ figures. Make sure all Markdown effects and tables have a white
background throughout all of the LaTeX files.

The draft is a scaffold: it lays out the full congressional amendment structure
(caption; A BILL; enacting clause; SECTION 1 short title and a clickable,
page-filling table of contents; SEC. 2 findings as the evidence-to-law-to-cost
record; SEC. 3 the operative amendment carrying new section 515D / 21 U.S.C.
360e-5 with its new financial-data record subsection and the ten conforming
amendments; SEC. 4 the comparative print widened to the user-fee section; and
the new SEC. 5 financial core - financial data transparency, device user-fee
treatment under section 738 (21 U.S.C. 379j), the authorization of
appropriations, and the Statutory PAYGO budgetary-effects clause). For the five
appendices (A cost estimate and budgetary data; B verification economics; C the
financial-data transparency standard; D the financial research-influence
matrix; E development transparency and commit schedule), it provides bracketed
[DRAFTING INSTRUCTIONS] that name the exact upstream files to process and the
figure or table medium each slot will carry, rather than the finished prose.
Every section and appendix must appear in the SECTION 1(b) table of contents.
Include the "Authorship, Notices, and Provenance" block and the References back
matter (Rule 3). The scaffold must compile in Overleaf exactly as committed.

The bill must be (a) up to date with today's medical-AI and federal budget law,
(b) grounded in current, mass-adopted financial and medical-law references used
meaningfully (the Statutory PAYGO Act, House Rules XXI, the MDUFA user-fee
sections, 21 CFR part 54, Open Payments, the GAO budget glossary), (c) relevant
to emerging bills only as research influences (kept out of the operative text
and confined to Appendix D), and (d) streamlined to the structured amendment
format.

One commit each for `main.tex`, `usctitle.sty`, `references.bib`, and
`README.md`; one commit per `sections/*.tex` file (each corresponds to
`main.tex`). Define sections by the figures and tables they share. Commit and
push in real time; do not hold commits. For the second-to-last commit, fix all
errors across all files. For the last commit, write the directory README
(repository structure, badges, gray-scale Mermaid diagram, table of contents)
and the `draft-bill-LaTeX.zip` Overleaf bundle. All commits belong to stage
milestone M6 of the single pull request.

Formatting (centralize in `usctitle.sty`, merged from the v3.0 and v4.0
styles): white background everywhere; RaggedRight body with even interword
spacing and no right-margin overflow; maximal widow and orphan penalties so no
single line is stranded and no one- or two-word last line is left behind;
correct symbols such as "SS" into "§"; single hyphens only (no en, em, double,
or triple dashes); every table column uses
`>{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{<width>}` (the `L`/`ragcol` helpers), the
flexible `Y` column, or the new right-aligned `R{<width>}` numeric column for
dollar amounts, set to `\textwidth`; use `\clearpage`, `\vspace`, and `\hspace`
as a senior author would to balance each page. The cover page keeps its context
but closes with a centered ASCII overview of the v5.0 financial build process;
condense the House and Senate statements; date June 9, 2026.

Create `auto-bill-02/draft-bill/prompt-draft-bill.md` (this prompt verbatim
under a `## prompt-draft-bill` heading) and `output-draft-bill.md` (the
narrative output). Do not stall, ask questions, or go into plan mode. The LaTeX
must compile in Overleaf with pdfLaTeX.
